steps.title: Compare the study methods
steps.content: The example is about why the difference between the maximum cougar densities reported by Sweanor and Choate might be artificially large. If one method systematically produces a lower density estimate, then a gap between studies using different methods could be exaggerated.

steps.title: Keep the cause relevant
steps.content: Choice A is about where radio-collar tracking is practical, not whether it changes density estimates. Choice C would make the helicopter estimate too low instead. Choice D discusses how common a method is in Utah, not whether the reported density difference is inflated.

steps.title: Pick the undercounting method
steps.content: The remaining choice explains the artificially large difference by saying radio-collar tracking may underestimate cougar density. The answer is B.
